review_content: I had a very pleasant stay at the Marriott Hotel Jabal Omar. From check-in to check-out, everything went smoothly. The hotel is well managed, comfortable, and the overall organization is excellent. The staff truly deserve special praise — everyone was professional, kind, and attentive. I would especially like to mention Yaser Mansoor from the breakfast team, who went above and beyond in taking care of guests and ensuring everyone felt welcome. His service really stood out and reflects very positively on the hotel. However, I must highlight one major issue that caused significant inconvenience. The adhan system inside the hotel was not working for two days, and there was no communication or notice provided to inform guests. As a result, I had to rely on going directly to Masjid al-Haram to ensure I did not miss prayers. For a hotel in this location, serving pilgrims, this lack of communication was a serious problem and should be addressed. Aside from this issue, my experience was excellent, largely thanks to the outstanding staff. With better communication around essential religious services, this hotel would easily offer a near-perfect stay. Value Rooms Location Service Sleep Quality

review_content: Blessings from Allah SWT! This is my first visit to Saudi Arabia. Makkah was the first city I stayed in for my Umrah with my family. The Jabal Omar Marriott Hotel truly makes my religious stay complete with no negative comments. The staff cater to all your Umrah needs making sure the stay is pleasant and facilitates in every manner possible so that one is able to carry out Umrah at any time of the day. The staff are very friendly, courteous and dedicated to their work, so much so one feels the tranquility being in this holy city. This hotel prepares one for their Umrah/ Haj, making sure their guests are supported in every way possible. I also celebrated my wife's birthday and the Umrah was her birthday gift. Furthermore, the hotel guest relations staff, Najd, prepared a trolley with cakes, balloons, Arabic coffee, dates ect, to make the celebration with my family and auspicious occasion. This was truly a very kind complimentary gesture from the hotel which made the day. The cleaning staff, Eman, Md Salim, Ibrahim made sure the rooms were cleaned thoroughly everyday. Other staff like Ali at the buffet reception, Marion Zamzami at reception were so kind and catered to our family needs. Praise Allah SWT for such a lovely stay. Love this hotel and would stay here again. You should too if you come to Makkah. Insider tip: Just make your request at the reception, they will help you 100% within their capability. Transport to the mosque and back is provided with less than a minute's wait.

review_content: Me and my family stayed here and had such a lovely time whilst visiting Makkah. They had transport which is free and apart of the hotel to take you to Masjid Al-Haram or if you prefer, it is just a minute or two of a walk. We had partial Al-Haram view from our room and we absolutely loved it. The staff were so lovely and polite - I also met a lovely member of staff that worked at the food counter, her name is Aya. The most sweetest and kindest soul that I met in my entire stay in Saudi. I could praise her endlessly, for only having met her for such a short period of time she has left such a memorable impression. I hope management know and understand that she is such a valuable asset to the team. Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: This Marriott Hotel is around 1km from the Mosque, but there is a golf cart service. The porters, Mohammed Nayem and Saif Alislam were very helpful and offered valuable advice and tips to navigate the city. The arrival was efficiently managed, but the checkout time of 12pm on a Friday is extremely inconvenient, as it coincides with the most important prayer of the week, in the most important city for Muslims. Some concession (to 3pm) should be given for late checkouts, when requested by guests. I was denied this concession and I was deeply disappointed as I was given only 15 minutes to return to the hotel after the prayers ended, and complete the checkout. Restaurant staff were first class, especially Rizwan and Chef Ghulam, and they catered beautifully for suhoor when we wanted to fast on a certain day. Housekeeping were hugely accommodating and attended to unpredictable schedule and needs. For a family room, the size was more adequate but decor and finishings in general, were rather dated. Insider tip: Change dated decor. Water pressure is good but toilet paper stocks run out quickly Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: This hotel is stuck in a 1980s time warp. It is bland and luck-lustre with staff who are disinterested and lack enthusiasm. The only exceptions being the gentleman in The Atrium Cafe and the laundry service which was actually quite goid. The main restauramt is of such a poor standard compared to other Marriott hotels. How can you not offer low fat milk at breakfast? Why is there no soya milk? The whole place is bland and lack-lustre. The problem is that they have such a high turnover of Muslim worshippers for Umrah/Haj and there is scant competition so they don't really care about the service. Definitely worth giving this place a miss!! Insider tip: Definitely avoid this place

review_content: stayed at the Jabal Omar Marriott Hotel in mid-November with friends from all over Bosnia and Herzegovina. The reason was to perform Umrah. The location of the hotel is fantastic. It is only a few minutes walk from the Kaaba, and if you are unable to walk, you can use the free hotel shuttle 24 hours a day. Transportation is provided by small buses from which you can take an elevator to the Haram or by small open cars that drop you off not far from the Haram. The rooms are large, tidy and are maintained every day. The beds are comfortable. The bedding is sparkling white. They have a mini bar, iron, ironing board, hairdryer and clothesline in the bathroom (above the shower). Towels are changed every day and water, coffee and tea are brought. Thank you Bilal for your service. You can hear the call to prayer from the Haram in the room (a special button on the wall). Breakfast is buffet-style and there is something for everyone. There is a wide selection of seafood, freshly prepared eggs in countless ways, cooked dishes (which are not always spicy), at least two soups to choose from, countless sweets, fruit, pudding, detox water, etc. The staff is extremely friendly and helpful. There is a souvenir shop in the lobby that is not overly expensive. There is an option to buy a copy of the Quran that the staff will take to the Haram on your behalf. With the desire to be your guest again, I warmly recommend this hotel.

review_content: Not the Marriott we hoped & Paid for Stayed for the night of October 31 & November 1, 2025 Not the Marriott we hoped and paid for. While hotel lobby looks grand but one can’t help and notice the condition of the lobby carpet which is worn out and stained. Same in rooms. Carpet there was no better than rooms in any Motels. Went to front desk to exchange money and lady at the desk said that hotel is out of cash and I must go outside to exchange and that I will get better rate than the hotel. Not very professional approach Amenities in room and bathroom were good but microwave must be in the room. Duvet insert was discussing. See the pictures. Can’t believe that Marriott standard fall so much. Few bright spots during our stay. Breakfast was very good along with friendly staffs there. Location was close enough and there was free cart service for the guests which was nice touch. In my opinion hotel in need of renovation especially carpet in rooms and lobby. Reception desk is huge but mostly one to two staff which are not good enough for check in/out time. Parking is expensive. Insider tip: Check your Duvet

review_content: Impeccable experience luxury hotel spacious and very comfortable room We liked the location of the hotel ( golf car H24 to drop you off next to the masjid, the view of the hotel is on alharam Smells good everywhere the corridors Super friendly staff especially the gentleman at the restaurant Welcoming on the first floor very smiling and super friendly The beds are comfortable and wide for single beds. Cleaning Service nothing to say and breakfast is exceptional and varied (protein pastries soup oriental juice mezze varied fruits ..) You hear al adhan from the room you can pray at the hotel also there is a mosque Nothing to say Cons: negatives. The proximity to the hotel is a slight slope which can be a downside for the elderly (but there are golf cars available H24) No bcp staff at reception sometimes we have a little wait.. Cash payments should be avoided because they have no cash We wanted to call Welcoming from the room but it's not indicated in the room or on the landline phone There was a strange smell in the room when we went in we believe that c carpet.. The ubers or mountain bikes are not allowed to pick you up next to the hotel c consecrated only for the private taxi Finally we liked to know that it was possible to take a box of food from the buffet in the morning nobody told us unfortunately... Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: I would not recommend this hotel at all. I booked a room with one double bed, but at check-in the relations manager “upgraded” me to a room with four single beds. Traveling with my wife, this was completely unsuitable. When I asked to change the room, the staff denied any responsibility, even after I showed them my booking confirmation, and dismissively told me “maybe we can do something tomorrow.” For two nights, my wife and I had no choice but to sleep on separate single beds in a cramped room where even basic amenities like the mirror were blocked by beds. Only after repeated arguments did the manager finally change our room on the third night. I did not expect this from a big chain like Marriott. I was not staying for free, nor had I paid less money — I simply expected the room I booked and a reasonable standard of service. Sadly, this experience was extremely frustrating and disappointing. Insider tip: They will probably mess up your room arrangements and tell you that you have booked it wrong. Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: The stay at the Marriott was great. The hotel is 5 min away from haram (walking) with coffee shops around. There’s a free shuttle service going from the hotel to the haram. review_content: We really enjoyed the Welcoming from the staff who take care of the breakfast. In particular, we thank Ali (receptionist) Haroun (waiter) and Rizwan (chief of staff). Not to mention the various foods that the Hotel offers for breakfast, with a reasonable price. Obviously we also thank the cleaners (very respectful and helpful) and also the Hostess (who explained everything in a very understandable way upon our arrival). Then we appreciated the efficiency in mobile services such as the “golf car” that takes you to the “Haram” mosque (free) and the “bus” that takes you to a “mini market” (also free). In the rooms you hear when they call “the Hasan” and you can pray both in the room and down in the (hotel) mosque. If you come as a family and buy two rooms, they will not be divided but will have a door that allows the passage from one room to another. I highly recommend this Hotel and we think it was one of the best experiences we had here in Makkah. Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: I had a wonderful experience at this hotel in Makkah. The location is excellent – just 3 minutes to the Haram by the hotel’s free shuttle bus. The rides are frequent, and I never had to wait more than 5 minutes, which made attending prayers very easy and stress-free. The staff were warm, welcoming, and always ready to assist. My room was clean, comfortable, and well-maintained, providing a peaceful place to rest after a busy day. Housekeeping was prompt, and the amenities were convenient and thoughtful. Overall, I highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Makkah for Umrah or Hajj. With its quick and reliable shuttle, friendly service, and comfortable rooms, it’s the perfect choice. I will definitely stay here again on my next visit. Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: This was my seventh visit to Makkah, and I enjoy discovering new hotels each time. For this trip, I chose the Marriott — and it was truly a beautiful experience, even though I did have one hesitation before booking: the distance from the Haram including the slight uphill walk, which I’ll mention a bit further down. The hotel is luxurious, but what made the stay truly special was the genuine kindness and attentiveness of the staff. From Nader at Guest Relations to the teams at housekeeping, reception, and breakfast — everyone was incredibly warm and truly went out of their way to take care of us. Their presence added a human and heartfelt touch to this deeply spiritual journey. My room was spacious, peaceful — which is not the case in all hotels there — and came with a nice view of the Haram. The bed and pillows were very comfortable — just what you need after long hours spent at the masjid. The elevators were always available, even at prayer times — unlike in some other hotels where they take a long time to arrive. The breakfast was delicious and generous, including a large choice of homemade bakery, fresh fruit juices, and plenty of variety every morning. I initially had some doubts about the distance to the Haram, being used to hotels inside the Clock Tower, but it was not an issue at all. The walk takes between 5 to 10 minutes, depending on which gate you want to reach and if you use Golf carts and shuttle buses that are available 24/7, running frequently, reducing the walk to only few minutes. That said, I often chose to walk — the slight uphill path wasn’t a problem for me. A heartfelt recommendation for anyone looking for comfort, serenity, and sincere hospitality during their stay in this sacred city??. (By the way, I found that booking directly through their official website offered a better rate than on other booking platforms????)

review_content: It's amazing hotel, but I would pay extra 200 per night and book other hotel closer to the haram like jumairah or hilton suites or alabraj. Because I was unlucky when I came back from tawaf and saeey , was waiting for the bus for 5p min then ended up taking taxi , then I realised that the marriot driver took a break and the double tree driver covered his trips but with double tree bus without telling me that he takes to marriot. The receptionist on check in was focusing on asking me to buy breakfast , and didn't tell where is the left ciz she was interested in serving next customers, we ended up going around the hotel .Also on drop off the taxi drops you on the road , so it will be easier for him to leave , although there is a drop place on the left , also only 1 luggage stand in a quadruple room , only small water bottles , difficult to go to the shop and buy water , but the rooms are very large and spacious, bad smell in the toilets, I would not go there again for my next umrah

review_content: I had the pleasure of staying at the Marriott Jabal Omar Hotel during my recent trip, and I cannot recommend this place enough! From the moment I arrived, I was welcomed with warmth and genuine hospitality. The staff truly went above and beyond to ensure my stay was comfortable and memorable. A special mention goes to *Taif* and *Fatima*, whose attention to detail and kindness made my experience unforgettable. Taif greeted me with a smile and made sure that all my needs were met, offering excellent recommendations for things to do in the area. Fatima, with her thoughtful service and care, made me feel like a valued guest every single day of my stay. Their professionalism and eagerness to create a joyful experience really stood out. They truly embodied the spirit of hospitality, making me feel welcomed and at home from start to finish. I was particularly impressed by the personalized touches, which made my visit feel incredibly special. Again a huge thank you to Taif and Fatima for their hospitality—it’s rare to encounter such genuine kindness, and they truly made my experience unforgettable!

review_content: We stayed at the marriot in makkah this december we had a very pleasant stay. When we arrived at the hotel we had a very swift check in and were welcomed to a clean fresh room . we had a quad room and i was really happy that it was suxh a spacious room because usually quad rooms in makkah tend to be quite small.. we had no problems with our stay.THe buggy and shuttle service was the best thing about staying in this hotel the 24/7 service we would leave lobby and it would be there promptly perfect fir goung to the haram! The only thing i would say is it would be nice if the carpets in room were washed as looked a bit worn but that wasnt a huge issue! ENCORE UNE AUTRE FOIS, MILLE FOIS MERCI A TOUTE L'EQUIPE DE L'HOTEL MARRIOT ET SURTOUT Mr MAHMOUD KAMAR. CREDIBILITE EXTREME "LE MEME CONTENUE EN LANGUE ARABE" Peace be on you, God's mercy and blessings. I renew the impeccable greeting of Mr. Hammarby to his humanity and high creation. Please accept my respect and appreciation for your sovereignty and all your colleagues. Thanks for telling the person that I haven’t forgotten his human attitude to me. A thousand thanks and appreciation. review_content: The hotel is great the lobby/reception area is so beautiful and smells so nice. The room is spacious and the beds are very comfy, the bathrooms are luxurious and up to the highest of standards there is such a nice view and the shuttles to the masjid are right downstairs the shuttles run for a long time even in the night and if there aren’t any it is an easy walk. There was only a small issue with our visit but it was fixed very quickly with no issues. review_content: The hotel rooms were extremely dusty and are in need of urgent upgrades.My daughter fell very sick on the night we arrived at the hotel because of the dust in the room. She ended up with a throat infection and was unable to visit the Haram due to her sickness. Her entire visit was marred by the stay at this hotel. The rooms are not at all upto the standard of a luxury 5 star hotel. They weren’t cleaned properly till the last day of our stay after we complained several times. The bathrooms were not cleaned fully and left wet after mopping and the showers didn’t drain properly - inadequate bathroom supplies for 5 people. The beds were not at all comfortable and the pillows inadequate. The location was highlighted as a walking distance to the Haram, but it wasn’t made clear that it was an uphill walk and aged people will struggle to walk. We chose to stay at the Marriott, given the high standards the hotel chain has in other places. But the stay here was extremely disappointing and not upto the standard of a 5 star hotel. Zero value for the money that is charged by the hotel. Will not be staying here again and will not be recommending the Marriott to any one. Insider tip: Wouldnot recommend it to anyone- cost doesnt match the service and facilites. Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: I booked this hotel and i was met with rooms that are less than what i had booked. I booked two triple rooms 5 months in advance of me arriving in Makkah. When i arrived i was given 2 double rooms, One which had a roll in bed added to the room. When i brought this up with Brother Naseer at the Reception he replied back to me saying that the room is registered as triple rooms, (I have included the rooms given to me below). I requested a refund as i was frustrated with the rooms they were giving me. The receptionist confirmed a refund was going to be made and i am still waiting for the refund, this is 5 weeks later. Hotel.com have stated that Jabal Omar Marriot are not providing confirmation of the refund and are not responding. AVOID AT ALL COSTS! Insider tip: AVOID AT ALL COSTS Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: Average Service and experience Despite messaging and receiving confirmation from the hotel before we left Singapore to have the beds ready for the children, nothing was done. After 9 hours flight a 2 hour drive, nothing was done, nothing explained, and no information was provided. We got to the room, one sofa bed was broken, and they advised to sleep on the sofa. Nothing was advised about shuttle service, and today, calling 4 times to pick laundry and clean our room before we returned by 1pm, nothing was done. There is no desire to act or service, and I am a titanium member for life. Horrible ownership and accountability, and if you're here for umrah, this hotel will test your patience. I am really disappointed with this hotel, and we honestly will opt for a better option next time inshallah.

review_content: During my recent stay, I encountered a significant issue with the room. Upon entering, I immediately noticed a strong smell of cigarette smoke, which was highly unpleasant. As a non-smoker, this was a major inconvenience that negatively impacted my experience. I promptly informed the staff about the issue, hoping they could assist in either deodorising the room or offering an alternative. Unfortunately, their response was disappointing. The staff seemed indifferent to my concern and offered no immediate solutions. I decided to visit the reception to address the issue in person, but it was extremely busy, with a long queue of guests waiting. The waiting time was excessively long, and it seemed the reception was understaffed. By the time I reached the front desk, I was frustrated with the lack of support and attentiveness. Overall, the unresolved smoking smell and the unresponsive staff made my stay far less enjoyable than expected. I hope the management will address these issues to improve the experience for future guests. Value Rooms Location Cleanliness Service Sleep Quality

review_content: I am a guest from room no. 2101.I stayed at hotel from 11th April to 18th April 2024. First, big nice room with soft mattress and large bathroom with full bathroom supplies. Nice decoration. But first I am a little disappointed for I have to walk to haram for 10 minutes. But later I have known that hotel has bus to haram (24 hr service). So no worries to walk. Room cleaning service is good. All hotel stuffs are friendly , nice and polite. I have to go back to Jeddah Airport today. But my flight time is 10 pm. So I have to go 5pm from Makkah to catch the flight. So I ask receptionist for late check out. Actually the standard check out time is no later than 13:00 noon. But hotel let me stay at hotel until 4:00pm for late check out with no extra charges apply. What a convenient. If not I have to wait in lobby from 1:00pm to 4:30 pm for my taxi ??. Thanks to this hotel and may Allah bless on them. If Allah give me another chance to come back, I would definitely sure to stay at this hotel ??

review_content: One of the worst hotels ever, very bad management, extremely unpolite stuff, I booked a room in this hotel for one night through the phone to do my Umra rituals, I agreed with the employee that I will pay cash upon arrival, I had a reconfirmation with this agreement again two days prior my arrival, I arrived to the hotel to discover that my room was cancelled and they don’t have any availability, instead of finding any alternative for me, they kept ignoring me for almost two hours, I was fasting and they didn’t even offer water for me, luckily I called the Address hotel which is a brand new hotel right infront the Marriott and they offered even less rate than the Marriott. This hotel shouldn’t be ranked 5 stars at all. Thanks god I didn’t stay. Value Location Cleanliness Service

review_content: "A Pilgrim's Feedback: Enhancing Access and Views at a Sacred Site" Review of Stay in March 2024: Room for Improvement in Accessibility I recently stayed at this hotel in March 2024 with my family, including children, primarily drawn by its proximity to the Haram. While the location is indeed advantageous for pilgrims and visitors to the holy site, I feel compelled to share aspects of our experience that could be improved for future guests. Visibility of the Haram: One of the expectations when staying near such a sacred site is the ease of access and, ideally, the visibility of the Haram. It was somewhat disappointing to realize that a potential architectural adjustment, such as a higher level prayer area, could have offered a direct view of the Haram. This would significantly enhance the spiritual experience of guests. It would be beneficial to hear the hotel’s perspective on this matter. Access to the Haram: The primary concern during our stay was the accessibility to the Haram. The hotel does offer a shuttle service, but the journey involves a somewhat convoluted route: an uphill walk followed by a downhill slide to reach King Fahad Gate, or a shuttle ride to the backside of the Makkah Hotel, then taking an elevator from “G Level” to “C1” Level, and walking to WC no 6 to finally reach the gate. This process, especially with family and children, was not as convenient as we had hoped. A suggestion for improvement would be the introduction of a complimentary battery-powered golf cart service, potentially organized by a collective of the Jabal Omar Hotel Union, directly to the King Fahad Gate. Such a service would not only provide a more accessible and appealing option for guests but also showcase the hotel’s commitment to enhancing the guest experience. Conclusion: While the hotel’s proximity to the Haram is undoubtedly a major draw, there are areas in accessibility and architectural design that, if improved, could significantly enhance the spiritual and physical comfort of the guests. It is my hope that this feedback is taken constructively, as the potential for an unparalleled stay is within reach with a few thoughtful adjustments. Insider tip: Access road to this hotel is through one of busiest road of makkah with peak hours from 5 pm to 7:30 pm.

review_content: The hotel location is the biggest advantage. Just near Haram and just maybe 2 minutes walk. They even have a golf cart right in front of the hotel which drops you to right in front of the Haram. You could quickly get back between prayers and refresh before the next prayer. Room I got was very spacious with a huge area in the front. Rooms were very clean and neat. Fully equipped rooms with all amenities provided. Beds were comfortable. Linen were new. A nice work desk was available. Staff were attentive and helpful. Was quite quick to respond and attend to our needs. Restaurant food could be a little better but a good choice was available with variety of options. Overall a lovely place to stay. Value Location Service

review_content: One of the worst customer service experience received at this hotel. While booking I was told I would be given breakfast upgrade by the Marriott reservation due to my Gold Elite tier with them. I spoke to the guest relations at the hotel as well not once but 4 times calling them from the US to Saudi Arabia and confirmed. Once I got to the hotel, they totally declined and pretended they never spoke to me. Mr. Mufti who was the person I spoke to said I don't remember even when I showed him the call log. review_content: Terrible Experience. Un Recommended At All Regret to inform you that Marriot Jabal Omar caused detrimental effects to their customer. Marriot does not deserve a five star hotel rating given poor customer service, inadequate resolution plans for customer compliant and inefficient operational workflows. First Concern: Double Deduction of Accommodation Cost Accommodation cost has been duplicated. One already took place through Booking.com on December 23, 2023 and the duplicated cost took place on my departure date March 11, 2024. This is unprofessional and mounted financial stress and burden on the credit card. PLEASE REVERSE THE DUPLICATED DEDUCTIONS. Second Concern: Poor Customer Service (More Specifically Related To Receptionist / Housing & Cleaning Stuff) For 2 consecutive days, I called Marriott Jabal Omar more than 30 international calls to take my IPAD that I missed on the wardrobe of room number 619. Agents (namely Mawadah, Abeer, Razan, Daie) who picked up the phone were hopeless, useless without any cooperative manners. All agents refused to connect me with Marriott Jabal Omar Manager claiming that he is in a meeting (for 2 consecutive days). All receptionist agents provided conflicting and unreliable messages claiming that: • One of the agent visited the room by herself and she could not found the IPAD. • One of the agent called housekeeping stuff and they confirmed that IPAD is not on the wardrobe. • One the agent called lost and found department and they confirmed that IPAD is not at their jurisdiction. All of the above has been proven as lies and Untrustable. I called Marriott Jabal Omar land line asking them to connect me with room number 619. The guest who occupied the room following my accommodation picked up the phone and he informed me that he found in the wardrobe / closet: • IPAD • 2 Birth Certificate • 2 Pairs of Socks When I called Razan and rest of receptionist agents to inform them that my missed things were already in the wardrobe blaming them that they rotate me into vicious circles without a materialized efforts, care, attention to my initial request. Unfortunately, they neglected all of my customer service issues and they narrowed the discussed on how I approached the guest who lodged in room number 619 (following my accommodation). Third Concern: Poor Transportation Cars Hardened Walks To Haram Given Horrible Hill / ramp. The hotel does not provide adequate transportation cars / electric cars to avoid the horrible / intolerable ramp. Most of the times, such cars are not operating and not available. This is not a five-star service at all. Seeking an apology note with a proper compensation plan given multiple detrimental effects caused by Marriott Jabal Omar during my stay. Accommodation: March 7 till March 11 Room Number: 619 Insider tip: WORSE EXPERIENCE IN MARIOTT JABAL OMAR Value Location Service

review_content: Performing Umrah We stayed at the Marriott for over six years to perform Umrah from Wednesday, March 13th, to Friday, March 15th, 2024. When we arrived, the hotel receptionists, Razan and Abeer, warmly welcomed us and promptly gave us room number 1710 at 4 pm. Hanna, the leader of the reception team, was incredibly cheerful when she met us! Our room was comfortable and relaxing, which was much needed after our long journey and the busy traffic to the hotel. The prayer room on the third floor, facing Makkah, was very convenient. However, we have a suggestion: the hotel should provide more chairs for elderly people during prayer. The buffet food was exceptional, with a wide variety of delicious dishes, sweets, cold drinks, and everything else you could think of. The chefs at the Marriott did a great job! We must admit most of the hotel staff are helpful, knowledgeable, welcoming and hospitable to visitors of Mecca to perform Umrah and Hajj. We always recommend the hotel to our friends and family. Insider tip: Arriving in the morning during Ramadan is best for performing Umrah, as it's less crowded in the Haram.

I was first upgraded to a junior suite then to a corner suite with a full view of the Haram. review_content: I am currently staying at the hotel, and the service and treatment is not what is expected of a 5* hotel. Do not expect this “5* hotel” to be the same as you would expect in other parts of the world. Requested 2 days and multiple times for a hairdryer, actioned on the 3rd day. Arrogant male staff member with little to know English, belittling is as food/drinks cannot be consumed in grounds. Ignorant as cafe service time for food minimum one hour. Only restaurant and food is limited to 4 food dishes and only select cuisine once per day for lunch service. No ala cart menu. Requested ice cubes to room, came 2 hours later. Food and drinks finished by then. No information on laundry service - told it does not exist. Staff access your room without your permission, telephone lines are not connected and the staff at the front desk have the audacity to say instead of visiting front desk for request we are to use telephone. Still waiting for phone line to be connected 4 days in. Front desk staff as always messaging on mobile phone rather and expect you to wait before being served. Shuttle service to taking to the Haraam, does not take you to the Haraam, rather to a back road - quicker to walk. Insider tip: Access to main road to Haraam is up a steep hill, should avoid if mobility access is required.

review_content: Only two towels in the bathroom and half a toilet roll. Two bottles of water for tea, coffee and drinking. I phoned up room service for extra towels and water but was given only two more bottles of water. I suffer from allergies so when I closed the curtains all dust went up my nose and in my mouth. Most worrying thing that happened was when turning on the kettle smoke came out of the plug. Lucky I was standing next to it so that I could unplug the kettle. Miraculously the kettle still works. The large round carpet at reception looked dirty. Maybe time for a change. The furniture in room looks very dated. Reading lights loose. Breakfast was good. Air con worked well. First time in Marriot Mecca but not first time in Mecca. There are a lot better hotels with a lot better service in Mecca. Insider tip: Get to know the cleaning staff they can help out lots. Unfortunately we never came across any. Value Location Service

review_content: My family and I had a great time staying at the Jabal Omar Marriot Hotel during our time in Makkah. The room was spacious, nicely decorated and granted us a clear view of the Al-Haram Mosque. Service was excellent with the receptionist Karim answering all our questions and taking good care of us. The breakfast buffet was also delicious with several cuisines to try from. All in all we had a lovely time and I would recommend this hotel to anyone. Insider tip: The location was ideal because it was directly across the road from the Al-Haram Mosque. They provide 24 hour shuttle services and footpaths Value Location Service

review_content: The hotel is very well located in Mecca. The room is clean, they fo have a microphone in the room connected to the holy Masjid Al Haram Adhan, it was my favorite point of the stay. I did not like the view of my first suite, they immediately changed my room to another panoramic view. Also the city is very crowded however the hotel is peaceful and does not feel crowded. All the team has been kind and welcoming, highlighting Mohammed Abu Tahib and Karim at the check out has been helpful. Mohammed booked a taxi for me as i am a woman traveling alone. Everything was safe. I had a beautiful stay! I will be back in the same hotel! Insider tip: The hotel is located 10mn walk distance from Masjid Al Haram. They have shuttle as well but honestly by walk is really closed by!

review_content: This is a beautiful hotel located very close to Haram (350m from Gate 79 to the Shuttle bus stop). The Shuttle buses run continuously. We are a gold elite member of Marriott and were very pleased to find out that our room was upgraded to an executive suite with a beautiful view of the Haram ?? We stayed for 5 nights and really enjoyed our stay at this Hotel. Both the management and room service were very pleasant and responsive. We love our buffet lunch at Atrium Cafe as they had a lot of variety of delicious & fresh food, not to mentioned amazing desserts at a reasonable price (50SAR per person). We also found prayer mats at the two stores downstairs after looking at many stores in the clocktower area. We like that they didn’t try to do any price haggling. Thanks for such an enjoyable stay in Makkah. We will be back, Insha Allah. review_content: Location is good overall, made acceptable by the frequent shuttle bus. Downside of bus is that it gets very busy during prayer times and have to wait a bit for the next one when you could just walk it. You can take a shortcut through the Hilton to avoid the climb/heat as alternative. Location on a hill. Not easy for wheelchair users. Difficult to get on bus for chair users. Shuttle bus cuts distance to haram by half. Still a bit of walking to do that is on a hill*** Rooms are great and good hospitality. We got haram view rooms so was added bonus. Can only see the new extension fyi. Bit disappointed no hairdryer in room or iron. Reception service prompt and fast. Comfy comfy beds, like sleeping on a cloud esp after all the walking tiredness from the haram. Razors shaving kit in rooms are useless and don’t take any hair away. Nb: hotel doesn’t have barbers either so will have to go to the Makkah towers area to shave and complete umrah. Only disappointment really is the bread/pastry section in breakfast. Not once was bread fresh instead it was stale and hard and at least 1-2 days old. All pastries also hard and as if it has been in open air all day. Definitely not Marriott standard. Stayed in far cheaper places but bread was decent. Insider tip: Shuttle bus is busy during Salah times. Easy to just walk it down on foot. Can take a shortcut through the Hilton on way back to avoid climb

Staff were very welcoming and went out of their way to help us. Breakfast buffet was fresh and lots of options. Awatif and Dai at the front desk were so friendly and helpful. Also Shamsud Doha deserves a special mention, always smiling and ready to help. Thank you to all the staff at Jabal Omar Marriot Makkah. Insider tip: Shuttle buses run constantly, but be aware that just prior to and after Salah times they do get busy. Value Location Service

review_content: The rooms are abit dated and its a slight uphill walk to Masjidil Haram - am not sure if the shuttle bus makes a meaningful difference in the distance or ease of journey. We actually have no issues with dated rooms or walking to the mosque thats perfectly fine we came for pilgrimage. What we do have some criticism is with the sevice overall- 1. They gave us a room that smelled of smoke on a non smoking floor. It took a lot of back and forth and consistent pursuing to get a different room. 2- the Bonvoy counter was usually left unmanned and quite often only one or two staff was at front desk despite a stream of customers. 3- I went for breakfast when my husband was feeling really sick - I asked two staff members if it would be OK for me to take food up to him and was met with a disbelieving sigh with two different warnings (i) just one plate (as though I planned to smuggle three bags of food out) and (ii) only bakery items please. Goodness what a way to show concern for an unwell guest! I was offended. 4. In the upcoming to arrival it took me more than 10 attempts through the Bonvoy app and multiple emails to ask if I could make payment prior to arrival. We are loyal Marriott Bonvoy gold elite members because we believe in the service of the brand and could have chosen to stay in other establishments much closer to Masjidil Haram but chose the Marriot to stay loyal to our membership. For those who are used to the Bonvoy service around the world, this Marriot does seem really untrained, inefficient and not hospitable and if we ever return to do pilgrimage we will not be staying here again. For those who are lucky enough to get a non smoking room or experience being ill here then this may be a passable option. Insider tip: Try to book a king bed room in advance as it often gets booked Value Location Service

review_content: Spacious clean room with very comfortable beds, helpful staff, very close to Haram, 2 minutes to haram by non stop shuttles availability, 5 minutes walking distance to Haram. But I experienced a bad incident with the customer service line. One day I had food poison around 4PM, I stayed in room throwing up every few hours all night, next day noon time of 15th August I was extremely weak, on I called the customer service, a none arabic speaking person answered me, I told him" I had food poison and I didn't eat for 24 hours, am better now but weak. what is good for me in your room service menu to gain my strength ? he said " I will check with the chef then revert to you". he called me back saying that sorry we cannot recommend to you any food and you have to check with a doctor", I said " ok, do you know any doctor here who i can contact ? or do you have any number ? he said " no sorry I don't have" , I told him that I am a stranger in this country, again he said sorry he cant help. then we ended the call. He just abandoned me after all what I told him seeking his help.

review_content: Polite staff but total inefficiency How I wish I could have given this place a better rating but there were so many flaws encountered which really marred my stay. I am a lifetime Titanium Elite member and had called the hotel a day earlier to request an early check-in as I was there for barely one night only. The manager was very polite and she accepted it. Moreover, my joy knew no bounds when I saw on the app and on the website that she had even upgraded me to the Presidential suite! On my way to the hotel, my app even alerted me that the room was ready. I could not have been any happier. Upon arrival, the saga commenced. The lady at the check-in counter decided to downgrade my room to a regular suite room. Then she told me to wait in the lobby as they wanted to ensure that everything was ok in the room. We waited for an hour and my patience was running short by then. Finally, we were given our keys and we went to the room. It was a regular suite that was totally worn out. The worst part was that even in spite of making us wait for an hour there were so many things not present in the room. The safety despot box was locked and in spite of several calls, no one came to unlock it. The bathrooms were devoid of any amenities. There was no remote control for the TV. The telephone and the loudspeaker were not working. It's sad that the staff in this property are so polite and courteous but there seems to be a huge void in efficiency and action. The hotel is like 10 minutes away by foot to the accessible area of the haram but it is not a straight walk and can be very tiring and exhausting in the sweltering heat. The shuttle service is very efficient and literally runs up and down every few minutes. Makkah isn’t the same as before and even after you are dropped down by the shuttle you still have to walk a lot to reach the main area to perform tawaf. Value Location Service

review_content: We stayed for a week. Overall, it was a decent experience although ultimately overpriced during Umrah. The room was relatively small. We were able to add a third guest at a cost of 300 riyals per night which was reasonable but only a bunk bed was offered and it took the minimal space available. The bathroom sink was clogged during our entire stay despite repetitive requests made to the cleaning staff and on the Bonvoy Marriott app. The food experience is however terrible! Iftar at the restaurant is extremely overpriced for 250 riyals per person and unjustified for a food of poor quality and not tasty at that. The scheduling was also hectic. Iftar was only from 7 to 9 pm and Sohour from 1 to 3 am; meaning if one wanted to attend Taraweeh, they would not be able to eat by the time they came back from the Mosque and would have to wait until 1 am to sustain themselves, which is ridiculous. We were therefore eating at the coffeeshop reception which was offering a buffet for 100 riyals per person, which was still overpriced for what it was. Only a few items were offered and not replenished.

review_content: Despite the location being not as close to the Haram as other hotels , yet it had much to offer. Check in was smooth, the lady at the reception was fast. The suite was very nice and clean. It had speakers in the bedroom of the Haram prayers, which was fantastic. Bellmen/Concierge people were very decent and helpful. The bus was always there to take us close to the Haram. Food was alright, that's my only not so good point ( can't say negative though ). The bed was comfortable ... in excellent shape. Which is what you look for after performing Umrah to rest. Shower room was nice as well, with good toiletries. Surely recommended and definitely I'll consider staying in again. Value Location Service

review_content: I stayed in the hotel between 6-10 April, with a Ummrah group. The best and most important item is the staff members. I had to deal with a number of them on different levels varying between general manager to housekeeper. They all share the traits of high professionalism, readiness to help and support The room design is excellent and comfortable, food quality is excellent. The only point in the room is the bathroom supplies could be better (shower gel and shampoo). Location is good if you can walk on a ramp like road, Otherwise, old and other persons may use a wheel chair to reach the 24 hour . .running coaster to go to Haram

review_content: Amazing Ramadan stay 2023 I stayed for five days during Ramadan , instant status recognition, as an ambassador elite I got an upgrade upon checking inn and was offered a complementary iftar . The hotel and the rooms were very clean. Cleanness is a very serious matter at the hotel. The bed was super comfortable as a Marriott bedding should be. Iftar buffet was really impressive. I tried suhoor and was equally impressive. I tried the atrium mini buffet and it was excellent with the open atrium and Ramadan vibe. The hotel have two prayers halls for men and women and they were linked to Al haram by speakers , prayer halls were in 3 rd floor and they were easily accessible. Two shuttle buses were offered to al haram and they were very convenient and they were air conditioned. I have stayed in many hotels around al haram and honestly Marriott in my opinion is the best and I highly recommend it. I would like to thank mr waleed silan the manager and mr Omar from front desk and razan too. I would like to thank mr Mahmoud for the impressive iftar and suhoor buffets. And mr MANSOOR from Conciarge desk. And finally I would like to thank mr Abdul hakem and Abdul Rahman for their exceptional house keeping and cleaning services for the 20th floor. Thank you once again for the amazing experience and inshallah see you soon.

review_content: Performing Umra from 29/3 to 31/3 On our arrival at the Marriott hotel on Wednesday 29/3, Sami Alrehai and Hana from the Reception, as usual, welcomed us with cheerful smiles, making our stay at the Junior Suite so relaxing and comfortable. Overall, the services and facilities provided by the hotel were extremely good during our three-day stay, with the exception of room cleaning, in which we discovered that under the sofa beds were not cleaned at all, suggesting that the cleaners only cleaned what could be seen!! Also, room service was very slow this time because we ordered tea and coffee for visiting friends, which took more than half an hour to arrive. This is after I went downstairs and complained to the desk services!! Suggestions: 1- Provide a complimentary minibus service between the train station and Aysha mosque for Umrah performers who wish to perform Umrah more than once. 2- In each room, place a welcome bottle of Zamzam water with the hotel's logo. 3- As a farewell gift, include three bottles of 200ml Zamzam water imprinted with the hotel's branding logo. Insider tip: Double-check your flight and hotel booking particularly if you are catching up on the train back and forth from Jeddah to Mecca.

review_content: Check in is not easy, we went 4.30 pm and at the reception lady Razan Wes dealing us. After 20 minutes I ask everything is ok, she yes, yes. Then she told us our room is not ready, need one more hour. You go for umrah, when you come back room will be ready. Imagine when you are in travel for 14 hours of flight from Chicago USA and all the immigration and dealing with taxis drivers in Saudi Arabia and when you arrive at the hotel and someone say after 20/25 minutes standing at the counter your room is not ready at 5.00 pm. This makes us more angry. This was not acceptable. Our room was prepay and will be ready by 4.00 pm at check time. Suggestion This hotel is not ideal for families with young children, elderly and peoples with knees or back problems. This hotel has shuttle but shuttle will drop off at the hill. You need to walk down the hill to Haram and walk up the hill to catch the shuttle.

review_content: The hotel and service was very nice. We had breakfast included in our package and it was definitely well worth it, there's a large variety of food options. The hotel had shuttle bus service's which come very frequently and take you somewhat close to the masjid. The staff were very kind and friendly especially the cleaning staff and catering staff. Only downside, We were charged an additional 'authorisation fee' by the hotel on top of our full package fee. We were told by the hotel receptionists that we'd get this back within 2 weeks however almost a month after we're still waiting. We have tried emailing them but got no response or refund so this is very disappointing.

review_content: It was a gorgeous and adorable place very near to the Holy Mosque. Rooms were spacious and comfortable with the speaker installed in the ceiling and we were informed punctually about the times of the prayers ( with the adhan). The Holy Mosque was just few minutes walking distance away and even prayers could be performed in the room with the imam of the Holy Mosque. thanks to Rinad from guest relations she is excellent Amazing service and housekeeping was very polite and helpful. Azhar a house keeping staff helped us a lot on our stay. And overall, it was a very nice experience Special thanks to Azhar and Abdul Rehman for responding to our needs promptly. Value Location Service

review_content: Changed rooms due to a stomach churning sewage like smell after using sink/shower, which was there in second room as well. No iron. Iron board broken. Kettle wouldn’t work. Couldn’t get the room to warm up, temperature playing up and cooling the room. It’s not as easy to get to the haram as it’s made out to be even on shuttle bus especially if you are elderly or have elderly relatives with you, the walk up the hill up to the shuttle bus can be hard work for them. Got to give credit that They attended quickly to all problems, all the people who attended to any problems were professional and punctual and very polite. But these problems shouldn’t be here in the first place. Disappointed by the actual hotel but not the service.

review_content: Absolutely loved our stay at the Jabal Omar Makkah Marriott hotel - the property was extremely impressive, the Haram-view suite was spacious and had stunning views, the breakfast was solid, and the the staff at the hotel were absolutely phenomenal in their customer service, really making the stay even better! Sami, Maram, Bassam, and Khadijah were all tremendous in assisting us at the reception throughtout the stay, Riyas was really helpful taking us up to our room after check-in and at check-out, Ismail Ahmed and the housekeeping staff were top notch, and Rahmath was absolutely phenomenal in assisting me with a couple of issues regarding payment and receiving points on my American Express Marriott Bonvoy credit card. He really went out of his way throughout the week and focused hard on resolving my concerns - I am very grateful for his genuine assistance! Thank you Jabal Omar Makkah Marriott team for a memorable stay, I will definitely be back, inshaaAllah!

review_content: A spiritual pleasant stay It was a pleasant stay. Nice staff at reception , housekeeping and in breakfast lounge. But the concierge services was not good as it should be. The location is close to Haram. 24hrs shuttle service to Haram is available. Ambience and comfort is as expected except the additional beds should be improved. Parking is available on site with charge. The other good thing is the live prayer from Haram on speakers in hotel. Insider tip: Parking can be difficult to find in busy times.

review_content: Makkah Marriott Hotel offers accommodations in Mecca . Guests can enjoy the on - site restaurant . Each at this hotel is air conditioned and is fitted with a TV with satellite channels. Certain rooms include a seating area for your convenience . Extras include slippers , free toiletries and a hair dryer . There is a 24-hour front desk, hairdresser's, and gift shop at the property. Masjid Al Haram is only 6 minutes from Makkah Marriott Hotel . The nearest airport is King Abdulaziz Airport , 46 mi from the property . Insider tip: I recommend it for anyone

Ramadan Iftar was decent quality given the large crowds. Even as an Elite member i don't think I'll stay here again

review_content: First time I stayed here. Spent two nights during Ramadan. There is a shuttle service from hotel to Haram every 5 mins - it’s a two min bus ride but a steep hill so worth doing it on the shuttle. Room was good and spacious. However staff and restaurants were under par. On arrival check in was slow, more staff available but too busy on their mobiles or talking rather than deal with customers queueing up to check in. Restaurants at Iftar and suhoor were pretty poor service. Iftar served till 9pm so I walked in at 8:15 but by then a lot of the food dishes were empty and tables had not been cleaned - yet despite knowing this they still charge you full! No effort made to keep tables clean as people leave and re set for others who may come in later. Same thing happened at suhoor. Staff not really interested to help - I saw some staff actually focus on specific tables guests who seemed to have been staying at the hotel for a few days and were being well looked after. I recognise Iftar and suhoor have specific demands but surely it’s not too much to ask them to keep the tables cleaned and the food stocked up for those who come in later. My room was cleaned and as I came back up to the room the staff still on that floor were keen to tell me they had just cleaned my room (looking for a tip). However when I went into the room, yes it had been cleaned and the bed made but no new towels and no further shower gel etc! Premises are good as is location but I think the overall service during Ramadan is lacking for a Marriott! Hoping now that pilgrims are returning, hotel management will give some focus to improvement in this area.

review_content: Visiting Umra with my friend Hamad Due to Corona and after the absence of two years from visiting the pilgrims to Mecca, the Marriott Hotel continues the best service in receiving pilgrims in terms of hospitality and complete comfort in the rooms and the restaurant,, It provided a job opportunity for Saudis, which is a positive step, Employee Safwan Saeed provided a distinguished and respected service during holy month “Fatoor” during our two days visit from Thursday 4/8 to Friday 9/4 is a good example of this. On the return back home, we will recommend the hotel to our families and friends.

review_content: Great Hotel, with amazing staff and excellent rooms. We met Mr. Azhar, one of the staff members at Marriot and he made sure we had an excellent stay. He made sure we had everything. Everything was very smooth from the checkin to checkout. Mr. Azhar got us upgraded to an executive suite when had a little problem with our room. Insider tip: The hotel has regular shuttle to Al Khalil Courtyard which is about 2 minutes walk to the mosque. The shuttle itself is about a minute long.

review_content: This is a very new hotel, large in size with a grand lobby that is welcoming. The rooms are good size and the restaurant is large and well designed and decorated. However, the location of the hotel is slightly away from the haram although the hotel has a regular shuttle bus service that operate 24hrs. The ride is no more than 2-3 minutes and it drops you few hundred meters outside the court of the Masjid. The reception service was not helpful. We requested a king/queen size bed but were given a room with two single beds. Initially, we were given pre-occupied rooms which were not ready for us. We were given two ready rooms but with the wrong bed configuration. When we asked for a room with a king/queen size bed we were told by reception that the entire hotel does not have that. Of course, this was not correct. When we inquired further we were told that the suites have king size bed, but this option was not offered to us even though the hotel was mostly vacant. Our rooms did not have soup, floor mats, face washers or other basic but expected toiletries. We had to request for those and finally got them. The hotel lacked a welcoming feeling that one would usually expect. Overall, this is a new hotel that is very clean but lacks professional reception and room service. Rooms Cleanliness Service
